phrasal_verbs.meaning: give free of charge; reveal sth you are trying to hide
find sth or meet sb by chance
use as a source of money
get sth, especially sth that is hard to get
write all the necessary information on a cheque, etc
provide sth good, so that sth bad seems less important
save an amount of money for the future
save money little by little (for a specific purpose)

debt: owe sb a debt of gratitude
increase: an increase in sth (of a certain amount)

B.title: Original course practice
B.instructions: Complete each original item using the target language from this lesson.

across.clue: Taste testers consistently rated this coffee .... as the smoothest on the market.
Rising unemployment is the biggest .... challenge facing the new government.
Fifty euros for a leather jacket at the market? That's a real .... !
The .... of renting a flat in the city centre has risen sharply over the past year.
The cashier placed the banknotes in the .... and handed over my change.
The street market vendor only accepts .... — no card payments of any kind.
All winter clothing is on .... at the department store this weekend — up to 40% off!

down.clue: Students can get a .... of 15% at the museum café by showing their university ID.
The company launched a new range of eco-friendly cleaning .... last spring.
Always keep your .... in case you need to prove the date and price of your purchase.
After the concert was cancelled, every ticket holder was entitled to a full .... .
Excuse me, waiter, could we have the .... please? We would like to pay separately.
Customs officers inspect all .... entering the country to prevent smuggling.

E.title: Original key word transformation practice
E.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it keeps the same meaning as the first.

questions.sentence:
Mia began the class project before the lesson on Money and shopping. **FOR**
The class project ...... before the lesson on Money and shopping.
questions.answer: saving some money for/putting some money by for

questions.sentence:
Leo began the study group before the lesson on Money and shopping. **PROFIT**
The study group ...... before the lesson on Money and shopping.

Sara began the exam workshop before the lesson on Money and shopping. **DEMAND**
The exam workshop ...... before the lesson on Money and shopping.
questions.answer: little demand for/not much demand for

questions.sentence:
Tom began the online lesson before the lesson on Money and shopping. **DEBT**
The online lesson ...... before the lesson on Money and shopping.
questions.answer: to be in debt to/to be into debt to/to get in debt to/to get into debt to
